summary: Systems-thinking + simulation expert panel: why does gossip neither beat nor lose to the oracle ring, and what drove the apparent negative VoI

pattern.top_hypothesis: At 6 stations the gossip network's diameter is ~3 hops, leaving insufficient hop-decay to delay propagation beyond the pirate relocation window (~2500 ticks), so gossip saturates to CommonKnowledge at effectively the same rate as the global ring — the channel's desynchronization mechanism cannot express and ring ≈ gossip on pooled outcomes by topology, not by tuning.
pattern.diagnosis:
Three overlapping archetypes explain the three distinct observations:

1. LIMITS TO GROWTH (dominant for "why gossip ~= ring"): The gossip channel's intended mechanism is staggered desynchronization — different haulers hear at different times, draining the hot route gradually rather than all-at-once. The structural LIMIT is graph diameter. At 6 stations the maximum inter-station path is ~3 hops; at hop_loss_milli=150 the residual signal after 3 hops is 0.85^3 = 0.614 — still above avoidance threshold for high-value (6M ransom) robs. Combined with the origin-pier deposit and the 12-hauler dock-visit frequency, the system reaches 800+ permille saturation on 2/4 seeds and reads CommonKnowledge. CommonKnowledge is informationally equivalent to the global ring — every hauler knows at roughly the same time. The pooled medians (55.40M gossip vs 56.55M ring, a 2.1% gap) reflect this degeneracy. The spec pre-registered this as "risk #1" and named the fix as "map growth, not artifact changes" — the pattern analysis confirms that name mechanistically.

2. PHASE-LOCK (dominant for "why avoidance fires but produces no net gain"): Even where saturation is not yet complete, the median first-hearing lag (2669 ticks) and the pirate relocation period (~2500 ticks) are nearly identical. The gossip warning fires at a route the pirate has ALREADY begun relocating from. Avoidance-lag after first-hearing is 170-632 ticks, meaning haulers DO react correctly and quickly once they hear — but the pirate is no longer there. The avoidance fires into a vacuum, producing no robbery reduction and no welfare gain. This is not a propagation failure; it is a phase relationship failure. The pre-registered "lag vs lie-low timescale phase-lock" is the active mechanism, confirmed by the numbers.

3. CHAOTIC BASIN SENSITIVITY (dominant for s23 catastrophe): Seed 23 sits near a basin boundary between the Alive predator-prey regime and a predator-dominant collapse state. Under the ring arm, the global instant warning prevents any early-run robbery cluster from forming. Under the gossip arm, the first ~2669 ticks are unprotected (before saturation is reached), and if an early robbery occurs in this window, a reinforcing loop (R2: more robs → pirate wallet grows → pirate hunger resets → pirate stays active → more robs) briefly dominates before the starvation-balancing loop (B1) can correct. S23's gossip-arm outcome (74 robs, pirate 39.95M, hauler 4.95M) is this reinforcing loop running to completion before B1 prevails — a predator-dominant trophic collapse, not a propagation malfunction. The M-DEAD control confirming s7 peace (zero avoidance signal, still peace) proves basin membership is seed-determined; s23 is the mirror case where the basin tip goes predator-dominant.

THE OWNER'S INTUITION vs THE MECHANISM: The owner senses "the incremental value of money vs danger isn't worth it." The blind→ring delta (+56% pooled median, from 36.25M to 56.55M) falsifies this at the population level — information IS worth 56% when the counterfactual is no information at all. The real observation the owner is sensing is the ring→gossip delta (~zero). The correct reframe: the CHANNEL DIFFERENCE (the new realism of lag, locality, loss) has no behavioral expression at this topology because the channel degenerates into its predecessor. The world-price argument is a contributing factor — robbery at 2-20 cr per decision against 1-4M contract rewards makes the avoidance signal weakly worth acting on — but even at higher stakes, CommonKnowledge saturation would persist because the topology is unchanged. Both interventions (stakes and topology) matter, but in sequence: stakes addresses "is information worth acting on?"; topology addresses "can the channel express any differentiation at all?"
pattern.decisive_test: Expand from 6 to 10-12 stations (or add 2-3 relay nodes with no contracts, increasing graph diameter from ~3 to ~5-6 hops) and re-run the same 4-seed, 3-arm grid, holding all propagation parameters fixed. Prediction: saturation drops from 800+ permille (CommonKnowledge) to sub-300 permille (Localized), median hearing lag rises from 2669 to above 5000 ticks (now materially exceeding the ~2500 pirate relocation period), and the gossip-vs-ring pooled median gap grows from the current 2.1% to 15%+ as the channel's desynchronization mechanism begins to express. If the gap does NOT widen under the topology expansion, the phase-lock hypothesis is wrong and stakes are the primary lever — re-run OD-1 stakes sweep at the expanded map. This test is more decisive than any parameter-tuning probe because it directly tests whether the limit is structural (graph diameter) or parametric (signal strength).
anti_recommendations: Do NOT tune hop_loss_milli to increase per-hop attenuation at 6 stations: more attenuation in a 3-hop diameter graph will push borderline-significant (low-tier) robs into NewsDesert while high-value robs (6M ransom, always above sig threshold) continue to saturate — you trade one MediaReading pathology for another without solving CommonKnowledge for the dominant robbery class.

Do NOT add significance weighting inside the route_evidence accessor: this is a valuation crossing the world API boundary (the spec ruled this out for correct architectural reasons) and is functionally reward-shaping through the observation channel, not a world mechanic — it treats a propagation symptom with an incentive intervention.

Do NOT interpret the s23 gossip catastrophe as evidence the gossip channel is malfunctioning or that propagation needs to be sped up: s23 is a basin-sensitivity artifact; the channel propagated correctly. Speeding up propagation would complete the CommonKnowledge collapse rather than fixing the catastrophe.

Do NOT gate or evaluate the channel-equivalence question on the pooled 4-seed median: 2 of 4 seeds are near basin boundaries (PermanentPeace or predator-dominant tipping points) and their regime membership swamps any channel-quality signal; the pooled median is dominated by basin noise, not channel signal.

Do NOT run the stakes sweep (OD-1) as a substitute for the topology probe: stakes addresses whether information is worth acting on given that all agents have it at the same time; topology addresses whether the channel can produce differentiated agent knowledge at all. These are orthogonal questions. The planned OD-1 sweep is correct as sequenced — run it first, read the VoI panel, then bring topology data alongside to distinguish the two mechanisms.
pattern.confidence: Moderate-High overall. The CommonKnowledge saturation reading (2/4 seeds, 800+ permille) and the phase-lock numbers (lag 2669 vs relocation ~2500) are directly measured and strongly support the topology-limit and phase-lock diagnoses. The s23 basin-sensitivity interpretation is inferred from cross-arm patterns and the M-DEAD control; it would be confirmed by reading the s23 gossip chronicle for early-run robbery clustering in the first 3000 ticks. Main information gap: whether the 2/4 CommonKnowledge seeds are the PermanentPeace seeds (in which case CommonKnowledge is a consequence of the peace regime) or the Alive seeds (in which case CommonKnowledge is causing channel-equivalence in both regimes). This distinction changes whether the topology fix is needed for ALL seeds or only the Alive ones.

leverage.diagnosis:
MECHANISM (verified in code). The avoidance transfer function is the binding constraint, not the channel. `economy.rs:577-580`: score = reward × (1000 − min(count×150, 900))/1000. With evidence_penalty_milli=150 (config.rs:308), the penalty CLAMPS at count=6 (6×150=900), flooring any hot route at 10% of reward — never erased. Against the tier spread 1.0/2.3/3.9M (scenario.rs:51, PER_UNIT_BASE 200k × {1.0,1.15,1.3} × qty {5,10,15}), a tier-3 route out-scores a clean tier-1 route until its count exceeds the tier-1 count by ~5+. So the function is a coarse step with a hard ceiling reached at just 6 sightings.

Now overlay saturation: the instruments show 6/6 alerts reach >800 permille, CommonKnowledge in 2/4 seeds, first-hearing lag median 2669 ticks. With evidence_window=4000 (config.rs:307) and that saturation, BOTH the ring and the gossip buffer drive most hot-route counts into the clamped/flat region of the transfer function. Gossip and ring feed the IDENTICAL formula (economy.rs:565-575, one branch swaps the count source); they differ ONLY in the count value. The realism of gossip — lag, locality, loss — manifests as a SMALLER, NOISIER count than the ring's instantly-complete global log. But a smaller count only changes score where the transfer function has slope, i.e. count 1-5. Above that, ring's count=12 and gossip's count=7 both map to the same 900 clamp → identical score → identical route choice → identical earnings. That is the pooled tie (56.55 ring vs 55.40 gossip) with violent per-seed variance: the variance is the lag/locality NOISE leaking through near the count-1-to-5 knee on a few routes, not a systematic survival signal. The owner's instinct is half-right and the blind arm tells us which half: blind→ring is +56% (information vs none matters enormously), but ring→gossip is ~0 because the transfer function is already saturated in the band where the channel's realism lives. The 'incremental value of money vs danger' the owner senses is real but it is the FLATNESS of the avoidance curve above count 6, compounded by a small map that lets one global ring already see everything — not a money-vs-danger price imbalance per se.
leverage.top_hypothesis: The gossip channel adds ~zero earnings because the avoidance transfer function (count×150 penalty, 900 clamp at count=6) is a coarse saturating step that flattens out exactly in the count band where gossip's lag/locality/loss differ from the ring — so the channel's realism never reaches the slope of the curve that turns sightings into route choice.
leverage.decisive_test: Single highest-leverage measurement: re-run the 3-arm grid with evidence_penalty_milli LOWERED so the 900 clamp is reached at a much higher count (e.g. 30 → clamp at count=30 instead of 6), holding everything else identical, and read the per-seed ring-vs-gossip earnings AND robs delta. This is a Level-5 world-price change (the avoidance transfer function is a rule, not reward shaping — it prices danger into the hauler's own contract choice, lawful under PDR-0006). If de-saturating the penalty curve OPENS a ring-vs-gossip gap (gossip now under-avoids relative to the omniscient ring because its partial count rarely reaches the higher clamp), the transfer-function-saturation hypothesis is confirmed and lag/locality/loss become live variables. If the gap stays ~0, the binding constraint is upstream (map size / global-ring omniscience) and the honest fix is map growth, not the curve. Pair it with one window: report the count distribution per route at decision time (how often count lands in 1-5 vs >=6) — directly shows what fraction of decisions sit on the flat part of the curve.
anti_recommendations: DO NOT touch the ransom cap (6M) or cargo/tier rewards (1.0/2.3/3.9M) to 'make danger matter' — Level 12 parameter tweak, lowest leverage. The blind arm already falsifies a stakes problem at the population level: stakes drive the +56% info-vs-none gap, not the ring-vs-gossip tie. Raising stakes amplifies BOTH arms equally and leaves the channel inert.

DO NOT add slot caps / extend or shrink retention as the primary lever (Level 11/12 buffer tuning). Saturation is 6/6 at >800 permille on a 6-station map; tightening craft_gossip_slots (8) or station slots (16) fights the symptom. The spec already pre-registered this: 'the honest saturation fix is map growth, not artifact changes.' Buffer tuning to force scarcity is artifact-gaming a too-small world.

DO NOT introduce any per-tick reward bonus/penalty for 'reacting to gossip' or 'avoiding danger' — that is reward shaping, explicitly forbidden by PDR-0006. Avoidance must stay priced through the contract-choice score (a world rule), never through a shaped signal.

DO NOT speed up or slow down pirate relocation (2500) or rob_cooldown (600) FIRST to chase a timescale phase-lock. Delay structure (Level 9) is real and the spec flagged retention-vs-relocation phase-lock as the central tuning risk, but it is downstream of the transfer-function flatness — changing when counts arrive does nothing while the curve ignores count differences above 6. Test the curve before the clock.

DO NOT reframe the per-seed variance as evidence the channel 'works sometimes.' The M-DEAD control already proved s7's peace flip is the read-SOURCE swap perturbing a seed near the escort-ladder peace basin, not gossip content. Treating that variance as channel value would re-introduce a presolvability-style read of noise as signal.
leverage.confidence: Moderate-to-high on the mechanism. HIGH that the transfer function clamps at count=6 and is shared byte-identically across arms (verified economy.rs:577-580, 565-575; config.rs:308). HIGH that stakes are not the population-level cause (blind arm +56%). MODERATE that saturation is what pushes counts past the clamp in BOTH arms — this is inference from the 6/6-at-800-permille instrument plus the clamp arithmetic; I did not directly measure the per-decision count distribution, which is the main information gap. The decisive test closes exactly that gap: it both confirms the hypothesis and tells you whether the next lawful lever is the penalty curve (Level 5 rule) or map growth (Level 10 structure). Leverage ranking: penalty-curve de-saturation (L5, lawful, highest leverage for making realism matter) > map growth to 10-14 stations (L10, the spec's honest saturation fix, higher cost) >> relocation/retention timescales (L9) >> slot caps/retention/stakes (L11-12, advised against as primary levers).

simarch.top_hypothesis: The gossip-vs-ring pooled-median gap is ~zero because two PermanentPeace basin-flip crossovers (s7 peace dividend +19.2M, s23 Alive loss −44.75M) cancel in the median, masking a real retention-bleed problem: gossip delivers evidence with a median lag of 2,669 ticks against a 4,000-tick evidence window, leaving only 1,331 ticks of usable signal — shorter than the pirate's 2,500-tick relocation period — so the channel delivers stale or near-stale evidence that cannot drive persistent avoidance at this map scale.
simarch.diagnosis:
THREE mechanisms interact, in confidence order.

MECHANISM 1 — Basin contamination invalidates the pooled median (HIGH confidence, directly arithmetic). The reported ~zero gossip-vs-ring delta (55.40 vs 56.55M) is a cancellation artifact. Sort gossip arm: [4.95, 46.55, 64.70, 72.60]; sort ring arm: [49.70, 53.40, 63.35, 67.20]. The pooled medians differ by only −2.75M but are composed of two offsetting signals that are mechanically unrelated: (a) s7 gossip arm lands PermanentPeace (robs=6, hauler=72.60M) while ring arm stays Alive (robs=39, hauler=53.40M) — the +19.2M is the peace-dividend floor, not avoidance VoI; (b) s23 is the only matched-basin Alive-vs-Alive pair where gossip arm is dramatically worse (4.95M vs 49.70M, Δ = −44.75M). These two contributions nearly cancel in the median. The pooled read estimates an uninterpretable mixture of two attractor basins and two channels; it cannot measure channel quality.

MECHANISM 2 — Saturation collapses the structural advantage of gossip (HIGH confidence, derived from config parameters verified in media.rs and config.rs). With hop_loss_milli=150 (keep=850/1000) and sig_floor_milli=50, the per-edge transfer probability at hops=1 for a ~3M-micros alert is 297/1000 (29.8%). Against a 6-station map with roughly 8 dock edges per station per evidence window, the probability that at least one hauler escapes any given station's reservoir is approximately 1−(1−0.297)^8 = 94%. The measured outcome — 800+ permille of all craft reached — is exactly what this arithmetic predicts. Once saturated, both gossip and ring produce the same count at the ASSIGN read site (economy.rs:566-567: `buf.count_route_recent(route, tick, evidence_window)` vs `route_evidence.count_recent(route, ships.info_tick[crow], evidence_window)`). The channels are functionally identical at saturation. The gossip channel's distinguishing properties — locality, partial information, propagation lag — are all erased when every hauler hears every alert.

MECHANISM 3 — Retention bleed: gossip evidence arrives too late to persist through the next pirate relocation (HIGH confidence on the timing arithmetic; MODERATE confidence that this is the dominant Alive-arm mechanism pending basin-clean measurement). Measured median first-hearing lag is 2,669 ticks; P90 lag is 6,450 ticks; evidence window is 4,000 ticks (config.rs:307); pirate relocate_period is 2,500 ticks (config.rs:304). At median lag, a hauler holds usable evidence for only 1,331 ticks after first hearing — 1,169 ticks shorter than the pirate's relocation period. At P90 lag (6,450 > 4,000), the evidence has already expired before the hauler hears it: the hauler contributes to the escape metric but cannot act on the information. The ring delivers the rob tick at the next dock, so a ring-arm hauler's window starts at robbery+dock_delay and runs the full 4,000 ticks. On Alive seeds, this timing advantage makes ring systematically more useful than gossip, which is consistent with the s23 matched-basin outcome (ring 49.70M vs gossip 4.95M). The owner's intuition — the gossip channel is not yet a live gameplay variable — is correct; it is not about prices. The evidence arrives but expires before use.
simarch.decisive_test:
Run seeds 0–19 (or 0–29) across all three arms (blind/ring/gossip) using the existing harness; post-hoc filter to basin-clean seeds — those where all three arms land Verdict::Alive (no PermanentPeace in any arm). Compare ring-vs-gossip Δmedian on only those seeds. Cost: zero new mechanics, pure harness rerun with basin-verdict logging already in the diagnostics.

Pre-registered prediction: on basin-clean Alive seeds, gossip arm will be measurably WORSE than ring arm (negative delta), because retention bleed makes the channel adversarial rather than neutral at this map scale. If gossip arm is measurably better on basin-clean seeds, the saturation explanation is false and something else is blocking avoidance. If gossip arm is approximately equal on basin-clean seeds, saturation is the dominant mechanism (map is too small) and the fix is map growth, not channel tuning.

Secondary instrument on the same run: for each gossip-arm Alive seed, record per-hauler (lag_at_first_hearing_for_route, did_that_hauler_avoid_route_on_next_ASSIGN). If avoidance rate drops sharply for lags above evidence_window minus a typical trip time (~1,500 ticks), retention bleed is confirmed as the primary Alive-arm mechanism and the world-price fix is evidence_window expansion, not hop_loss or sig changes.
anti_recommendations: Do not change contract reward tiers or ransom prices. The blind arm falsifies the price hypothesis at the population level: information-off haulers earn 36% less than ring-arm haulers (36.25M vs 56.55M), so the marginal value of money vs danger is already priced in. Adjusting prices would mask the channel problem without diagnosing it.

Do not tune hop_loss_milli or sig_floor_milli downward to increase propagation speed. Saturation is already at 800+ permille of all craft. Reducing attenuation further pushes saturation higher, which makes gossip and ring even more equivalent. The problem is not propagation amplitude — it is that the map is too small for the channel's locality property to survive.

Do not expand gossip buffer caps (station_gossip_slots or craft_gossip_slots). Buffer eviction is not the constraint at this map scale: 6/6 stations are already holding alerts. Adding buffer slots does not change the hearing-lag distribution that drives retention bleed.

Do not increase evidence_penalty_milli. ASSIGN already avoids at 150milli per recent-rob count, clamped at 900 (config.rs:308, economy.rs:578). Sharpening the penalty on an already-functioning avoidance signal does not fix the lag-vs-window timing problem that makes gossip evidence expire before use.

Do not interpret the ~zero VoI gap as confirmation that the gossip channel is working correctly or that it is ready to be a gameplay variable. The zero gap is a measurement artifact. The channel is correctly implemented but is not yet live as a player-observable dynamic because saturation has collapsed its locality property on the current 6-station map.
simarch.confidence: Moderate-High overall. The basin-contamination arithmetic is airtight (direct calculation from the reported numbers). The saturation prediction is grounded in verified config parameters (media.rs, config.rs) and the measured 800+ permille escape rate. The retention-bleed timing calculation is exact given the reported median lag (2,669 ticks) and config defaults. The remaining gap is that the s23 matched-basin result is one data point and the gossip-arm basin verdict is inferred from rob count rather than directly reported. The basin-clean sweep (decisive test) resolves this gap cheaply and is the required next step before any world-price or channel change.

emergence.diagnosis:
The owner is sensing something true that the population medians hide: the gossip CHANNEL adds ~0 earnings over the ring (pooled 55.40 vs 56.55M) even though it changes WHO-knows-WHEN visibly in the chronicle. The mechanism is in the consumer, not the channel. The ASSIGN decision (economy.rs:577-580) is `score = reward x (1000 - min(count x 150, 900))/1000`, and ASSIGN picks the argmax score across available contracts. Three properties chain to kill the channel's earnings signal:

(1) It is a RELATIVE COMPARATOR, not a cost-benefit weigh. Evidence is a per-route DISCOUNT on reward, never a danger cost weighed against a wallet or an expected loss. Two evidence sources (ring vs gossip) produce identical picks whenever they produce the same RANK ORDER of contracts. So the channel's realism (lag, locality, loss) only matters where it flips the argmax — everywhere else it is invisible to earnings by construction.

(2) SATURATION collapses the rank orders together. The instruments say 6/6 alerts reach >800permille of craft, 2/4 seeds read CommonKnowledge, median first-hearing lag 2669 < retention 4000. So the window where an informed hauler's pick differs from an ignorant one's (lag ~2669) is SMALLER than the window where they agree (retention 4000 minus lag). By the time a route is hot enough to matter, nearly everyone has heard, so the gossip read converges to the ring read's ordering. This is exactly the LLN-over-a-homogeneous-field failure that killed the two prior NO-GOs — rebuilt one layer up: not self-averaging within a tick, but self-averaging across the POPULATION'S BELIEFS via saturation. The +56% (info vs no-info, blind 36.25 -> ring 56.55) is real because ANY avoidance beats none; gossip ~= ring because WHICH avoidance signal barely re-ranks picks once beliefs converge.

(3) The 900-clamp and the tier spread make the discount a within-tier-only lever. Penalty saturates at count=6 (6x150=900); one rob = 15% discount. A tier-3 route (3.9M) discounted 15% = 3.32M still beats an undiscounted tier-1 (1.0M), so information can only re-rank SAME-TIER alternatives. That is the same fact as the banked PPO ablation NULL (contact info worthless to a lone learner at 2-20cr/decision) — read from the population side.

The violent per-seed variance (gossip 72.6/4.95/64.7/46.55) is the tell: the M-DEAD control proves s7's peace flip is the read-SOURCE swap perturbing a seed near the escort-ladder peace basin, NOT gossip content. Gossip is acting as a content-blind coin-flip on which basin a near-critical seed falls into — the exact opposite of a felt, decision-relevant information resource.
emergence.top_hypothesis: The decision surface is a saturating relative comparator (rank-order avoidance), not a danger-vs-money cost-benefit weigh, so once saturation makes the gossip rank order converge to the ring's, the channel changes WHO-knows-WHEN (visible in the chronicle) without changing WHO-earns-WHAT — information is real but not yet decision-relevant.
emergence.decisive_test: Persistent information asymmetry is the single load-bearing variable, and the cheapest probe is a MAP-GROWTH sweep, not an artifact change: re-run the existing 4-seed grid at the spec's pre-registered honest fix — more stations / longer lanes so first-hearing lag and the hub/backwater news ratio (currently 2.5-3.0) widen past pirate relocation (~2500) and retention (4000) — and read the EXISTING value-of-information panel (media-live vs media-dead, both belief-ON) together with the saturation window. The decisive signature is: gossip pooled earnings pull AWAY from the ring (not just away from blind) AND the saturation reading drops out of CommonKnowledge into Localized on more seeds. If earnings separate only when asymmetry persists, H1 is confirmed and the lever is map size (a world mechanic), not stakes. As a confirming cross-check on the same runs, report the share of ASSIGN picks where gossip-count and ring-count would yield a DIFFERENT argmax — H1 predicts that share is tiny at 4 stations and grows with map size.
anti_recommendations: Do NOT touch the route_evidence accessor or add significance/recency WEIGHTING to the count — a weighted count is a valuation crossing the world API (the spec already rejected this as a CRITICAL); it would manufacture an earnings delta by shaping the comparator, not by making information scarce.

Do NOT raise ransom_cap or rebake stakes as the FIRST move. Stakes (H2) is real but secondary — the blind->ring jump (+56%) proves avoidance already pays at the population level, so the channel's flatness is a saturation/comparator problem, not purely a price problem. Higher ransom also feeds pirate metabolism and is the owner's console-baked calibration (OD-1: sweep axis only, owner rebakes after reading).

Do NOT give pirates gossip reads or couriers to 'make it matter' (OD-6). The interdiction-RL NO-GO showed information-chasing predators self-equalize risk and destroy the very heterogeneity that makes hauler information valuable; prey-that-knows vs predator-that-hungers is the watchable asymmetry.

Do NOT add a detour/convoy/escort-on-demand consumer yet. The deferred ladder is two-sided (generators AND a consumer that can act); a new consumer needs the in-flight detour verb that lands with maydays. Adding consumers before the asymmetry exists just gives more mechanics the same saturated, rank-converged signal to read.

Do NOT chase the per-seed variance by tuning toward higher pooled medians — the M-DEAD control already proved that variance is content-blind basin-perturbation, so 'fixing' it by retuning would be tuning to an artifact of the read-source swap, not to gossip content.
emergence.confidence: High on the mechanism (H1): it is read directly from economy.rs:577-580 (relative argmax comparator + 900-clamp) against the measured saturation instruments (>800permille reach, lag 2669 < retention 4000, 2/4 CommonKnowledge) and is corroborated by the M-DEAD control and the banked PPO NULL. Medium on the RANKING of the remedy: the smallest cut that creates a visible haves/have-nots arc is almost certainly map growth (the spec's own pre-registered honest fix for saturation risk #1), but I have not measured the lag/asymmetry curve as a function of station count, so the exact map size that drops seeds out of CommonKnowledge is unknown. Main information gap: no run yet isolates the fraction of ASSIGN picks where gossip-count and ring-count disagree on the argmax — that single number would directly quantify how much of the channel is currently dead weight versus live, and would convert this diagnosis from inferred to measured.
